chunk_summary: This section outlines same-day referral criteria for emergency comorbidities in overnutrition and obesity management, emphasizing continuity of care and specific pathways for urgent cases.
learning_objectives: Identify trigger symptoms and objective findings requiring same-day referrals in obesity-related emergencies.
Understand referral pathways, including emergency department, specialty clinics, or direct admission.
Apply guidelines for managing diabetic ketoacidosis, hypertension, and SCFE in pediatric patients.
Ensure continuity of care by documenting emergency presentations effectively.
key_facts: Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) and hyperglycemic hyperosmolar state are critical emergencies requiring immediate referral (ISPAD 2022).
Hypertension in children is defined as blood pressure ≥95th percentile for age, sex, and height (AAP 2017).
SCFE (slipped capital femoral epiphysis) is a common orthopedic emergency in obese adolescents, requiring urgent orthopedic evaluation (POSNA guide).
Eating disorders, such as anorexia nervosa, may present with severe electrolyte imbalances necessitating same-day care (NICE NG69).
Continuity of care is critical to avoid repeated screenings and ensure effective follow-up after emergency presentations.
reference_quote: ISPAD clinical practice consensus guidelines 2022: diabetic ketoacidosis and hyperglycemic hyperosmolar state — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36250645/
document_key_topics: Emergency referral criteria for obesity-related comorbidities
Management of pediatric hypertension and DKA
Continuity of care in urgent obesity management
